//: Discussion on how to write comments in package.json:
name: browserhive

//scripts.build: Compile TypeScript via `tsc -b` (composite build — picks up tsconfig.generated.json through the `references` array in tsconfig.json). Output goes to dist/. The `prebuild` lifecycle hook runs `openapi:prep` first.
//scripts.test: Vitest test runner in single-run mode (see vitest.config.mts for setup). The `pretest` lifecycle hook runs `openapi:prep` first so generated TS is fresh.
//scripts.lint: Top-level orchestrator that runs both `lint:source` (ESLint) and `lint:openapi` (Redocly). Has no `prelint` hook — see the `pre*/post*` notes below.
//scripts.server: Run the compiled HTTP server entry point (`dist/bin/server.js`). Requires `npm run build` to have produced the dist/ output.
//scripts.lint:source: ESLint over the TypeScript sources. Backed by `prelint:source` (= openapi:prep) so the generated `src/http/generated/*` files are fresh before eslint type-checks them via eslint-import-resolver-typescript.
//scripts.lint:openapi: Redocly lint over `src/http/openapi.yaml`. Independent of code generation — runs against the YAML directly, so no `prelint:openapi` hook is needed.
//scripts.openapi:prep: Composite of openapi:generate + openapi:gen-operations + openapi:bundle. Extracted so prebuild/pretest/prelint:source do not duplicate the chain. Redocly lint is intentionally NOT part of this chain — it is a contract-quality gate exposed as `lint:openapi`, not a code-generation prerequisite.
//scripts.openapi:generate: Runs @hey-api/openapi-ts (configured by openapi-ts.config.ts) to generate TypeScript types, an operationId-keyed SDK, and a fetch-based client from src/http/openapi.yaml into src/http/generated/.
//scripts.openapi:gen-operations: Generates src/http/generated/operations.gen.ts — a typed OPERATIONS map (operationId → { method, path }) and OperationId union — from src/http/openapi.yaml. Must run AFTER openapi:generate because hey-api cleans the output directory before writing.
//scripts.openapi:bundle: Pre-dereferences src/http/openapi.yaml to dist/openapi.dereferenced.json so the runtime server can wire up Fastify Ajv schemas without depending on @apidevtools/swagger-parser or the source YAML at runtime.
//scripts.openapi:build-docs: Renders the Redoc HTML used as a static, externally published artifact (GitHub Pages workflow). Not required by the runtime server — `/docs` and `/openapi.yaml` are no longer served by the application.
//scripts.docs:preview: Local one-shot preview: builds the same Redoc HTML the CI publishes, then prints the absolute file:// URL. Open it in a browser. Self-contained — no local web server needed. (`redocly preview-docs` was removed in @redocly/cli v2; `redocly preview` requires paid Reunite packages and is not used here.)
pre*/post* hooks: Scripts named `pre<name>` and `post<name>` are npm lifecycle hooks:
when you run `npm run <name>`, npm automatically runs pre<name> first,
//scripts.prebuild: npm lifecycle hook for `build` — runs `openapi:prep`. Fires automatically; see the `pre*/post* hooks` note.
//scripts.pretest: npm lifecycle hook for `test` — runs `openapi:prep`. Fires automatically; see the `pre*/post* hooks` note.
//scripts.prelint:source: npm lifecycle hook for `lint:source` — runs `openapi:prep`. Fires automatically; see the `pre*/post* hooks` note.

scripts.lint: npm run lint:source && npm run lint:openapi
scripts.openapi:prep: npm run openapi:generate && npm run openapi:gen-operations && npm run openapi:bundle
scripts.docs:preview: npm run openapi:build-docs && node -e "console.log('Open file://' + require('path').resolve('dist/docs/index.html') + ' in your browser')"

//dependencies.fastify: Fast, low-overhead HTTP framework with native JSON Schema validation (Ajv).
//dependencies.ajv: JSON Schema validator. Pinned at the root so transitive resolutions land on v8 (matches Fastify's bundled compiler).
//dependencies.puppeteer: Headless browser library. Used at runtime via puppeteer-extra to connect to remote Chromium servers.
//dependencies.puppeteer-extra: Puppeteer wrapper that allows plugins. Used with stealth plugin to avoid bot detection.
//dependencies.puppeteer-extra-plugin-stealth: Stealth plugin for puppeteer-extra. Applies various evasion techniques to avoid headless browser detection.
//dependencies.yaml: YAML 1.2 parser used by examples/data-client.ts to read data/*.yaml fixtures (replaces the previous CSV format so entries can carry inline comments).

//devDependencies.@apidevtools/swagger-parser: Pre-dereferences openapi.yaml at build time (scripts/openapi-bundle.mjs). The output JSON is shipped instead of the YAML, so this is build-time only — not required at runtime.
//devDependencies.@hey-api/openapi-ts: Generates TypeScript types, operationId-keyed SDK functions, and a fetch-based client from openapi.yaml. Output is src/http/generated/. Replaces openapi-typescript + openapi-fetch (one tool for both type and runtime client).
//devDependencies.@redocly/cli: OpenAPI linter / preview / docs generator. Configured via redocly.yaml at the repo root; surfaced via `lint:openapi` (a sibling of `lint:source` under the top-level `lint` orchestrator) and the openapi.yml CI workflow.
//devDependencies.@types/estree: Required for TypeScript type resolution. Transitive dependency from eslint and rollup (via vite).
//devDependencies.@types/json-schema: Required for TypeScript type resolution. Transitive dependency from @eslint/core.
//devDependencies.@types/node: Required for TypeScript type resolution. Transitive dependency from vitest and vite.
//devDependencies.@types/yauzl: Required for TypeScript type resolution. Transitive dependency from puppeteer via extract-zip.

puppeteer-extra-plugin-user-data-dir > r: puppeteer-extra-plugin-stealth depends on user-preferences -> user-data-dir -> rimraf@3.
rimraf@3 pulls in deprecated glob@7 and inflight@1, causing npm ci warnings.
The puppeteer-extra monorepo is unmaintained since March 2023 (GitHub Issue #914).
We override rimraf to the project-level v6 ($rimraf) to eliminate these warnings.
rimraf v4+ has a breaking API change (Promise instead of callback), but this is safe
because we disable the stealth user-agent-override evasion in src/browser.ts,
so user-data-dir's rimraf call (deleteUserDataDir) is never executed at runtime.

//devDependencies-rimraf.rimraf: Only used as the override target for puppeteer-extra-plugin-user-data-dir. See //overrides for details.
